title: Nebula
notes: You are on the Planet NEBULA during the time of the Pharaohs in 2019. A thousand of pitfalls await you and the Gods see you when you approach their tombs. Can you use your laser beams and homing bombs to destroy them? Make no mistake, because it will be fatal! Contains four levels for one or two players.

title: Doraemon
notes:
Doraemon is an arcade platform game based on the anime/manga of the same name. You control the titular character, a blue robotic cat from the future with a phobia of mice, over three stages of play.

In the first stage, your time machine has been stolen and you and the gang (Nobi, Shizuka, Gian and Suneo) have to search a building and locate all of the parts. You walk or fly around the different levels of the building, avoiding enemies that appear from some of the doors. There are also mice that scurry around the screen, which will cause you to freeze in fear if they touch you. Occasionally, your sister Dorami appears with a bonus item, which will give you the ability to dispose of enemies (boxing gloves, air jets, etc) or make you invulnerable to enemy attack for a short period of time. Some of your kid friends will also appear from behind the doors, carrying parts of the time machine if they find them. Once you have collected all ten parts, the stage ends. You use the time machine so that you and the kids to escape from the building, but something goes wrong and you end up in the prehistoric past...

In stage 2, you have to avoid pterodactyls, sauropods and cavemen with throwing weapons, find the kids and bring them back to the time machine. Once all of the kids are collected, you can escape from the past. But something else goes wrong, the time machine breaks into parts, and you find yourself in the distant future. In stage 3, you have to both find the missing kids, and collect the missing pieces of the time machine, all while dodging UFOs and what look like robotic crabs. Once this is completed, you return back to your usual time. You are then given a bonus game, where you solve 15-tile slide puzzles, after which the game loops from Stage 1.

title: Super Baseball
notes: Super Baseball has a relatively orthodox system for an early baseball game, and the basic operation method is not much different from that of subsequent family stadiums (there are also changing balls and ball speed adjustments). It has some pioneering features as a baseball game of the pre-Family era.

title: Elevator Fight
notes:
Elevator Fight is an original arcade platform game in a style similar, but not identical, to Elevator Action.

Arin, your space suit-clad hero, must travel downwards in shaft of 31 floors populated by dangerous robots, space mice and aliens and filled with traps, in order to destroy the central computer of the Gashim empire on the bottom floor. The shaft is divided in a multitude of floors, connected by lifts. Both you and your enemies may use the lifts, using them both to advance, to escape and to ambush. The enemies have different behaviours, with some rushing toward you and some shooting. Traps include grappling claws and lasers in the ceilings and electrical floors. For defence, you have a gun.

While your character can jump across gaps in the floor, he is not an invincible platform hero and will die from falling through the floor. One way to cross a wider gap in the floor is to use a transport rail in the ceiling, something which requires precision. In order to complete a section of the shaft, you must collect the necessary keys, so you cannot rush through a section, thereby avoiding enemy contact.

title: Mappy
notes:
You control Mappy, a police-mouse whose job is to collect valuables (consumer electronics) from a cat's house. You are being pursued by a gang of five pink cats. Doors are your only defense against the cats. You get from floor to floor by jumping on one of the trampolines.

Points are earned by recovering stolen goods. Other ways of earning points are bouncing on a trampoline, putting cats in the microwave, striking a cat with a door, striking cats with a bell, retrieving a treasure from the Boss cat Goro or dropping cats through a hole in the floor.

title: Boulder Dash
notes:
You are Rockford, and you have to dig through monster infested caves in search of diamonds. In each level you must collect a certain number of these diamonds, in order to open a portal to the next stage. Enemies can be squashed by falling boulders, which are released when the ground below them is removed or they are pushed onto empty ground, but be careful because these can also squash you. In later levels, difficulty is increased by many puzzle elements and shorter time limits.

There are 16 levels to be completed and five different skill levels available, which affect the number of jewels which must be collected and the time limit.

title: Lupin Sansei
notes: This adventure takes place successively in an underground, in a cave, and in the jungle. You are Lupin and you run in search of diamonds. But beware, crocodiles, vultures, snakes and tree trunks are chasing you. Either jump over them or destroy them with your fist. During the fourth phase, you are in a room in which you have to shoot at moving targets in order to be able to escape. So hurry yourself and don't waste a minute, because time is precious. For one or two players.

notes: You play the part of Bounty Bob, and it is your mission to explore every inch of an abandoned mine. As you walk over floor sections in the mine, the floor will change color. When all of the floor sections have been changed in color, you can move on to the next, more challenging level. To reach all of the floor sections, you will need to figure out how to get there.

title: Pop and Chips
notes:
The adventure of Pop and Chips begins with the fairy Pippi, who has been kidnapped by Pumpkin the Great. There are blocks that can be broken and blocks that cannot be broken on the screen. When you break the fruit block, Pippi appears and moves up and down on the screen, and when you go around and back to the original position, he returns to the original fruit block. If you touch Pippi, he will be collected at the top left of the screen.
Once you return to the fruit, the movement will be faster from the second time. Clear the screen by collecting all the Pippi.

title: Sky Kid
notes:
You are the legendary Red Baron, flying a plane through a horizontal side-scrolling scenery set during World War I. To complete a mission, you simply need to get to the landing spot on the other side without being shot down, but you can gain extra points by destroying enemy vehicles and planes. For huge points, pick up a bomb hidden in the level (you will be warned by a sound when approaching it) and drop it on a large structure. If you fail to land on the landing strip, you run out of fuel and crash.

You can only shoot horizontally and diagonally by tilting the plane's nose. When in trouble, perform an aerial loop with the secondary button, this often destroys other planes and avoids bullets. There is a co-op mode with the character Max as well.

images.clear_logo: Images/Epoch Super Cassette Vision/Clear Logo/Sky Kid (Japan) (En).png

title: Astro Wars II: Battle in Galaxy
notes:
Astro Wars II: Battle in Galaxy is a space shoot-em-up sequel that reuses a lot of the same graphics and gameplay as the original Astro Wars. You control a rocket that moves back and forth along the bottom of the screen, and can shoot forwards at aliens to complete levels. As in the first game, each level consists of two stages. The first stage is to fight off a mother-ship with a horde of smaller vessels, while the second is to defeat the mother-ship. The levels then repeat until you run out of lives.

Your ship and the alien mother-ship appear to be identical to the first game, but other details differ. The first stage in this game features a vast array of swooping and bombing aliens, closer to Galaxian instead of the army of para-troops that moved down the screen in Space Invaders style from the first game. The second stage has the mother-ship spewing out zig-zagging lines of ships to attack you instead of individual erratically moving missiles.
rom_name: Astro Wars II - Battle in Galaxy (Japan) (En)

title: Kung-Fu Road
notes: M. CHEN, master of KUNG FU, hates the members of the TUE family, made of brutal and unscrupulous personas. Learning that his fiancée's father has been killed by the TUE Chief, he burns to avenge her. Thanks to his mastery of KUNG FU, help Mr. CHEN get to his enemy's castle, having triumphed over his henchmen, vase throwers, giants, steel stars and bulls all standing in his way. Then defeat the TUE Leader to win. For 1 or 2 players. Also includes a possibility of combat between 2 Players.

title: Super Golf
notes: The course has 18 holes and a total length of 5,805 meters. You have to get the ball into the hole with a minimum of strokes, despite the wind and many obstacles. You can choose the club, the direction and the force with which the ball will be sent. Challenge your friends, one to four people can play together.

title: Star Speeder
notes: A 3D course with simple graphics like thin blue sleepers lined up in outer space, and spacecraft race on an inexhaustible course (16 faces in total) like a roller coaster. In addition to enemy planes, foreign objects called fantasy graphics may enter the course. Starts are individual rather than simultaneous, competing for running time. The time of the enemy aircraft is set in advance, and if you are in the 3rd place, you can proceed to the next race.

notes: Find yourself facing your opponents on a football stadium! You can dribble, pass, shoot, or send the ball with your head. You can control the trajectory of the ball and the length of each pass. You can also shoot corners, free kicks and penalties. For one or two players.

title: Y2 Monster-Land
notes:
In order to rescue the heroine Naoko who was kidnapped by Monster Land who got lost in the amusement park, Tooru's great adventure begins with a cross in one hand.

Monster Land consists of 6 areas and unique monsters await, from Zako enemies called mini monsters to strong enemies called big monsters. If you follow the road on the map, the order of capture of the area is free to some extent, but you can not rescue Naoko unless you search all 6 places. Hidden items and hidden rooms may be found by hitting blocks or hitting the cross. In the hidden room, characters from other SCV games may appear depending on the conditions.

notes: This is the 60,000km race, one of the three biggest races in America, Africa and Europe. Let's tear off with full power on-road and use wheelie technique off-road. Your guts and timing will be the deciding factor.

title: Punch Boy
notes: Armed with a mini-punch, the main character, Punch Boy, goes through a maze of various gimmicks to Monster Castle, defeats four bosses and saves the captured Lady. In the maze, monsters are born from rock-like eggs (which can be rolled with a punch). The monster does not accept mini punches from other than the front, faints with the mini punch from the front, and can be defeated with another blow. Some eggs do not hatch, and monsters and punch boys often roll eggs.

title: Dragon Ball: Dragon Daihikyou
notes: The first Dragon Ball video game ever produced. It was released by Epoch, making it the only game not to feature any kind of involvement with Bandai or the subsequent Namco Bandai. The game is an overhead shoot'em up that allows players to take on the role of Goku who rides on the Nimbus while firing Ki blasts and swatting at enemies with his Power Pole.

title: Shougi Nyuumon
notes: The objective of the game is to take the opposing king. When the king cannot avoid being caught on the next move, the game is over. They say the king is "Matt". The shogi opposes two players who take turns playing. The Shogi chess board is called Shogi Ban ("Shogi table") and has 81 squares (9 x 9), the three rows furthest from the players constituting their promotion area. At the start of the game each player has 20 pieces: a king, a rook, a bishop, two gold generals, two silver generals, two horsemen, two spears, and nine pawns.

title: Dragon Slayer
notes:
Dragon Slayer is an early example of the action role-playing game genre, which it laid the foundations for. Building on the prototypical action role-playing elements of Panorama Toh (1983), created by Yoshio Kiya and Nihon Falcom, as well as Namco's The Tower of Druaga (1984), Dragon Slayer is often considered the first true action role-playing game. In contrast to earlier turn-based roguelikes, Dragon Slayer was a dungeon crawl role-playing game that was entirely real-time with action-oriented combat, combining arcade style action mechanics with traditional role-playing mechanics.

Dragon Slayer featured an in-game map to help with the dungeon-crawling, required item management due to the inventory being limited to one item at a time, and featured item-based puzzles similar to The Legend of Zelda. Dragon Slayer's overhead action-RPG formula was used in many later games.

title: Astro Wars: Invaders from Space
notes:
Astro Wars is a space-based shoot-em-up. Your base is under attack by aliens. Their mother-ship is unloading hordes of fighters to smash through your shields and disable all eight of your defending spacecraft. There is no time to waste. You must shoot down the attackers and limit the damage to your base. Then, once the situation has stabilized, you must take your remaining spacecraft in pursuit of the fleeing mother-ship.

During the chase stage, you will be bombarded by fast zig-zagging missiles. The mother-ship can be destroyed by a well placed shot, but if you can't manage that soon enough, it will escape. Either way, you will have time to repair your shields and replenish your fleet before the alien's inevitable return with more capable equipment. Once you lose all of your spaceships in one of the raids, then the game is over.
rom_name: Astro Wars - Invaders from Space (Japan, France) (En)

title: Comic Circus
notes:
Comic Circus is a variant of the earlier Exidy arcade game Circus which involves clowns trampolining around the screen popping balloons. In this game, while there are balloons to be caught, the main aim is to catch some doves that have escaped from their cages.

There are three levels of play. The first involves a teeter-board and two clowns. You control the position of the teeter-board to catch each clown as they fall. Missing a clown will lose a life, but a successful catch will cause the other clown to rocket into the air. While in the air, the clown can pop balloons for points (if they are high enough) or catch a dove in flight. Sometimes, a dove will drop a bonus which lies on the floor for a short period of time and can be collected by the ground clown. In two player mode, the players alternate positioning the teeter-board.

The second level is similar to the first, but two clowns are holding a trampoline while the third is in flight. The third level has some added trapeze artists swinging across the top of the screen. Catching the doves in the corners of the screen requires the clown to be carried across the screen from one acrobat to the next. Cut-scenes of the clowns chasing the doves are shown between levels. After all three levels are complete, the game loops.

title: Milky Princess
notes:
Milky Princess was a pack-in game with a particular version of the Epoch Super Cassette called "the Super Cassette Vision Lady's".

There are two game modes. The goal of the first "game" is to predict the future, in other words it's about astrology. In the second game, you can maneuver Milky Princess freely around the screen.  Symbols of the zodiac appear on screen and if they touch her, you lose a life.  Floating in from the top of the screen are flowers and your goal is to collect all of them. As you collect them, the field on the bottom fills up and when it's complete, you move on to the next stage, which is a little bit faster.

To give credit to the title, Epoch linked the name of René Van Dale Watanabe (a famous astrologer) to the game.

notes: In the sequel to Pole Position, you are again driving an F-1 racer in a timed qualifying run, trying to achieve the pole position. Then it is off to the race, in hopes of taking that top spot. The difference in Pole Position II and it's predecessor is that now you have four tracks to select from.
